title: philadelphus microphyllus var. argyrocalyx
intro: Philadelphus microphyllus var. argyrocalyx is a lesser-known variety of the mock orange plant, a flowering shrub native to China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs and sore throats, and promoting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, although it is worth noting that the scientific evidence supporting its medicinal uses is limited.
